The General Staff of the Kuwaiti Armed Forces announced on Saturday morning that its air defenses are actively engaging with missile and drone threats amid the ongoing treacherous Iranian aggression. It added that any sounds of explosions that may be heard are the result of ongoing interception operations of hostile missiles and drones.

The General Staff of the Kuwaiti Armed Forces announced on Saturday morning that its air defenses are actively engaging with drone threats amid the ongoing treacherous Iranian aggression. It added that any sounds of explosions that may be heard are the result of ongoing interception operations of hostile drones and urged the public to be calm and follow safety and security instructions issued by relevant authorities.

Dubai hosts the 11th edition of the Knowledge Summit on 17–18 November 2026 at the Dubai World Trade Centre, organised by the Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum Knowledge Foundation (MBRF) in partnership with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP). The summit will bring together leading experts, decision makers, and innovators from around the world to discuss rapid global transformations and the role of knowledge in delivering practical solutions that support sustainable development.

RT by @moiuae: RT by @moiuae: The Government of Dubai Media Office (GDMO) confirms that the Reuters report regarding explosions in Downtown Dubai is false. GDMO urges the public and media to rely only on official sources for accurate information and to avoid spreading rumours or unverified information.

flydubai announces the introduction of a second daily flight to Bangkok from 18 July. The additional service comes just weeks after the launch of the carrier’s daily flights to Don Mueang International Airport (DMK) on 01 July. With the start of its second daily service to Bangkok and the increase of its Krabi service from four weekly flights to daily operations, flydubai will operate up to 21 weekly flights to Thailand, expanding its presence in the market.
